nodes.label: Perception References (Vibrotactile Findings)
nodes.rationale: Well-established vibrotactile findings grounding every encoding choice; the difference between guessing and designing.

nodes.label: Vibrotactile frequency: peak 200-300 Hz, Weber ~20-30% (poor pitch)
nodes.rationale: Useful tactile vibration ~10-1000 Hz, peak sensitivity ~200-300 Hz (Pacinian corpuscles); frequency discrimination poor (Weber ~20-30%), skin resolves maybe 5-9 distinct buzz pitches; a one-fret ~6% change is unfeelable as frequency. Tactus starting pulse 160 Hz.

nodes.label: Two-point discrimination (fingertip 2-4mm, forearm 35-40mm, torso/back 40-50mm)
nodes.rationale: Cited generic anatomy (Weinstein-style): fingertip ~2-4mm, palm ~10mm, forearm ~35-40mm, torso/back ~40-50mm. Consequence: keep actuators >=4-5cm apart; put higher-resolution frets on torso, coarser strings on back.

nodes.label: Temporal acuity ~5ms gap detection (skin's strength) -> sharp pulses + sweep
nodes.rationale: Gap/successiveness detection ~5 ms; skin times far better than it pitches; use sharp pulses (30-80 ms) not drones; render strums as a timed sweep low to high so a wrong note feels like a wrong spot in the sweep.

nodes.label: Funneling / sensory saltation (separate simultaneous cues across regions)
nodes.rationale: Two near-simultaneous stimuli close together merge into one perceived locus; fire simultaneous cues on separated sites, never adjacent.

nodes.label: Neosensory Buzz (Eagleman) — 4 wrist actuators, learnable code
nodes.rationale: Encodes audio onto 4 wrist actuators; users learn to discriminate words. Lesson: few well-placed actuators + a structured learnable code beats a dumb pile.

nodes.label: Precedents: Model Human Cochlea, SoundShirt, Braille / the vOICe
nodes.rationale: Model Human Cochlea maps audio frequency bands spatially across the torso; SoundShirt does torso vibrotactile music for Deaf audiences; Braille / the vOICe prove structured sensory-substitution codes are learnable.

hyperedges.label: Perception findings jointly ground the location+timing encoding
